description: Create a content slide on roles evolving most in risk management, with a left narrative sidebar, a center horizontal bar chart of skill and role changes, and right-side commentary plus a CRO quote.

header.headline: Roles evolving most – upskilling, automation of routine work and new hybrid roles

left.body: As AI absorbs a greater share of routine and analytical tasks, CROs expect the greatest changes in upskilling, redesigning routine work, and creating new hybrid roles that combine domain risk expertise with AI and data capabilities.

center.title: Figure 18: Over the next three years, which skills and roles within your risk management team do you expect to evolve most significantly in response to AI?

series.label: AI vendor and service provider reliance

items.text: For risk leaders, workforce strategy can no longer be separated from technology strategy.

As automation and AI absorb a greater share of routine and analytical work, CROs must deliberately redesign roles, upskill teams and rebalance capacity toward judgment, insight and business partnership.
The ability to translate productivity gains into stronger resilience and enterprise value will increasingly define second-line effectiveness.
What CROs say: In the future, essential skills for risk professionals will include AI literacy, data governance and the ability to communicate complex challenges clearly to diverse stakeholders.

footer.source: 15th annual EY/IIF global bank risk management survey
